generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GreenhouseEnvironmentActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Virtualized actuators controlling greenhouse environmental parameters via pre-recorded VM images for adaptive feedback loops"
]

instance AirQualityVentilationController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualitySensorDataStream
    base:desc "Control logic for ventilation systems based on AutomationML standards for air quality management"
]

instance GantryCranePositionController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    DTDFVocab:producedData GantryKinematicDataStream
    base:desc "Hardware/software unit managing real-time kinematic operations for lab-scale gantry crane with position feedback and force regulation"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance GreenhouseTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData GreenhouseEnvironmentDataStream
    base:desc "Environmental sensor for greenhouse monitoring with implied accuracy of ±1°C"
]

instance GreenhouseHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData GreenhouseEnvironmentDataStream
    base:desc "Environmental sensor for greenhouse humidity monitoring"
]

instance GreenhouseCO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData GreenhouseEnvironmentDataStream
    base:desc "Environmental CO₂ sensor for greenhouse monitoring with implied accuracy of ±100 ppm"
]

instance AirQualityCO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualitySensorDataStream
    base:desc "Raspberry-Pi based CO₂ sensor (SCD30) with ±100 ppm accuracy for air quality management"
]

instance AirQualityTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualitySensorDataStream
    base:desc "Raspberry-Pi based temperature sensor (DHT22) with ±0.5°C precision for air quality monitoring"
]

instance AirQualityHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualitySensorDataStream
    base:desc "Raspberry-Pi based humidity sensor (DHT22) with ±2% RH precision for air quality monitoring"
]

instance AirQualityPM25Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualitySensorDataStream
    base:desc "Particulate matter sensor (PM2.5) with ±10-30% accuracy for air quality management"
]

instance AirQualityPM10Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualitySensorDataStream
    base:desc "Particulate matter sensor (PM10) with ±10-30% accuracy for air quality management"
]

instance GantryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData GantryKinematicDataStream
    base:desc "Encoder for position tracking of gantry crane puck/container with implied precision of ±0.1mm"
]

instance GantryForceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData GantryKinematicDataStream
    base:desc "Force/torque sensor for crane load monitoring"
]

instance GantryProximitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    DTDFVocab:producedData GantryKinematicDataStream
    base:desc "Proximity sensor for collision detection along gantry crane axes"
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance GreenhouseEnvironmentDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GreenhouseTemperatureSensor, GreenhouseHumiditySensor, GreenhouseCO2Sensor
]

instance AirQualitySensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualityCO2Sensor, AirQualityTemperatureSensor, AirQualityHumiditySensor, AirQualityPM25Sensor, AirQualityPM10Sensor
]

instance GantryKinematicDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GantryPositionEncoder, GantryForceSensor, GantryProximitySensor
]

// C5: Data Storage
instance GreenhouseTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series environmental data stored in InfluxDB with high granularity from pre-recorded VM datasets"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GreenhouseEnvironmentDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBDatabase
]

instance AirQualityCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Cloud-processed air quality data from Raspberry-Pi sensors via Azure IoT Hub with 100+ Mbps transmission"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirQualitySensorDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AzureIoTPipeline
]

instance GantryRealTimeData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "High-frequency kinematic data (kHz range) from gantry crane sensors for real-time validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GantryKinematicDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

// C10: Models
instance GreenhouseEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric and analytical model for greenhouse environmental parameters using pre-recorded VM images and sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBDatabase, ActiveMQBroker
]

instance AirQualitySimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model-driven air quality simulation using AutomationML for spatial sensor placement and behavioral modeling"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AutomationMLStandard
]

instance GantryKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Closed-loop kinematic model for gantry crane with inverse/forward kinematics and predictive validation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance InfluxDBDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for high-frequency environmental data storage with temporal coherence"
    DTDFVocab:enables GreenhouseTimeSeriesData
]

instance ActiveMQBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time message broker enabling ActiveMQ-mediated event handling for adaptive feedback loops"
    DTDFVocab:enables GreenhouseEnvironmentModel
]

instance AzureIoTPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based processing pipeline for air quality data from Raspberry-Pi sensors via Azure IoT Hub"
    DTDFVocab:enables AirQualityCloudData
]

instance TimescaleDBDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for high-frequency kinematic data storage with real-time validation capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables GantryRealTimeData
]

instance AutomationMLStandard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automation Markup Language for standardized sensor data integration and interoperability"
    DTDFVocab:enables AirQualitySimulationModel
]

instance MATLABSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kinematic simulation tool for modeling gantry crane dynamics and control algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:enables GantryKinematicModel
]

instance PythonLoggingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based logging application for aggregating sensor data into structured logs"
    DTDFVocab:enables GantryRealTimeData
]

// C6: Services
instance GreenhouseAdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Self-adaptive control service for greenhouse environmental parameters using real-time InfluxDB/ActiveMQ pipelines"
    DTDFVocab:provides GreenhouseEnvironmentInsight
]

instance AirQualityVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runtime visualization service for air quality monitoring using AutomationML and Azure pipelines"
    DTDFVocab:provides AirQualityPredictionInsight
]

instance AirQualitySimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation service for air quality spread prediction using AutomationML models"
    DTDFVocab:provides AirQualitySimulationInsight
]

instance GantryRealTimeValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous validation service comparing real-time sensor data with simulated trajectories (kHz frequency)"
    DTDFVocab:provides GantryPositionAccuracyInsight
]

instance GantryFaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Machine learning-based fault detection for crane components using kinematic models and real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:provides GantryAnomalyDetectionAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance GreenhouseEnvironmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral adaptation insights for greenhouse climate control adjustments based on real-time sensor data"
]

instance AirQualityPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Runtime predictions of indoor air quality conditions based on sensor data and AutomationML models"
]

instance AirQualitySimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated air quality spread patterns for virus prevention strategies"
]

instance GantryPositionAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation accuracy metrics comparing real-time sensor measurements with simulated trajectories"
]

instance GantryAnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic fault isolation and response actions triggered by anomaly detection in crane operation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance GreenhouseEnvironmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive environmental adjustments based on real-time greenhouse monitoring data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance GreenhouseEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GreenhouseTemperatureSensor, GreenhouseHumiditySensor, GreenhouseCO2Sensor, GreenhouseEnvironmentActuator, GreenhouseEnvironmentModel
]

instance AirQualityZone : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains AirQualityCO2Sensor, AirQualityTemperatureSensor, AirQualityHumiditySensor, AirQualityPM25Sensor, AirQualityPM10Sensor, AirQualityVentilationController, AirQualitySimulationModel
]

instance GantryCraneSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryPositionEncoder, GantryForceSensor, GantryProximitySensor, GantryCranePositionController, GantryKinematicModel
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **digital-to-physical interaction** in the gantry crane system is mediated through the **Control Application** and **Human Machine Interface (HMI)**. Key components include:

- **Command Types**:
  - **Trajectory Commands**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** produces optimal movement trajectories (e.g., Cartesian or polar coordinates) with zero residual swinging motion, sent via the **Physical Crane Interface** to the crane’s controllers.
  - **Operator Commands**: The **HMI** (Python frontend) allows manual overrides or configuration adjustments (e.g., speed limits, path corrections).
- **Control Message Formats**:
  - Likely **structured binary or JSON-based** commands (e.g., JSON over MQTT or binary protocols like Modbus/TCP) for low-latency execution.
  - The **Control Application** translates high-level Python logic into hardware-specific commands (e.g., PWM signals, motor commands).
- **Validation and Authentication**:
  - **Pre-command Validation**: The **Continuous Validation Service** may validate trajectory feasibility (e.g., collision avoidance) before execution, though this is not explicitly tied to the control flow.
  - **Feedback Systems**: The **Physical Crane Interface** likely includes **closed-loop control** (e.g., PID controllers) to confirm command execution (e.g., position feedback loops).
- **Conflict Resolution**:
  - Operator commands (via HMI) may override automated trajectories if safety thresholds are breached (e.g., velocity limits exceeded).
  - The **Trajectory Generator Service** prioritizes optimal paths, while the **Control Application** ensures adherence to physical constraints.
- **Command Execution Confirmation**:
  - The **Logging Application** records successful command executions and their outcomes (e.g., achieved position vs. target).
  - The **Continuous Validation Service** compares actual vs. simulated trajectories post-execution to validate control performance.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs **real-time behavioral adaptation** with **synchronized updates** between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. The system relies on **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage, which inherently supports high-frequency sampling (e.g., **1-second or lower granularity**) for real-time monitoring. The **ActiveMQ message broker** facilitates **low-latency event-driven communication** (sub-millisecond to milliseconds) between sensors, the physical system, and the digital twin components. Pre-recorded virtual machine images ensure deterministic deployment, but no explicit synchronization frequency is specified beyond real-time adaptation loops.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **Azure-based runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction**, where **sensors (Raspberry-Pi + custom hardware)** feed data into the digital twin at **real-time or near-real-time intervals** (e.g., **10-second to 1-minute intervals** for predictive analytics). AutomationML-based modeling enables **model-driven synchronization**, but no detailed latency metrics are provided. The system’s **prediction layer** operates on **historical data** (not real-time) for long-term forecasting, implying asynchronous processing for certain components.

No explicit **latency requirements** or **temporal granularity specifications** are detailed in the provided documents for the lab-scale gantry crane or other exemplars. Thus, **twinning_time_scale** is inferred as **real-time to near-real-time** for adaptive systems (GreenhouseDT) and **real-time to batch processing** for predictive systems (Air Quality Management), with **no standardized synchronization frequency** specified beyond general terms like 'runtime' or 'adaptive loops'.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs a **self-adaptive digital twin constellation** where both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin exhibit **behavioral adaptation** via **InfluxDB + ActiveMQ**. The **orchestration** is **ad-hoc**, relying on **containerized virtual machines** (pre-recorded images) for deployment and **message broker-driven event handling** for synchronization. **Resource management** is implicit in the **virtual machine setup**, but no **explicit orchestration patterns** (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker Swarm) or **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., Zookeeper, Consul) are specified. The **component integration** follows a **modular, event-driven architecture**, with no standardized **digital twin framework** (e.g., Siemens MindSphere, PTC ThingWorx).

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **AutomationML-based digital twin modeling** integrated with **Microsoft Azure tools**, enabling **model-driven orchestration** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction. The **orchestration pattern** is **centralized** (Azure pipeline), with **coordination** via **Azure IoT Hub** (implied) and **AutomationML’s semantic interoperability**. **Resource management** is abstracted through **cloud-based services**, but no **decentralized twin coordination** (e.g., blockchain, peer-to-peer) is mentioned. The **component integration** leverages **Azure’s IoT and ML services**, with **no explicit twin constellation patterns** beyond the AutomationML model.

The **lab-scale gantry crane** case (C4 Model) suggests a **centralized twin constellation** with **software architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, event sourcing) for **containerized deployment**. The **orchestration** would rely on **C4-compatible tools** (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) but lacks **explicit twin coordination mechanisms**. The **resource management** is implied by **containerization**, but no **cyber-physical twin federation** or **multi-twin governance** is detailed.

Thus, **dt_constellation** is **ad-hoc for GreenhouseDT** (InfluxDB + ActiveMQ + VMs) and **model-driven/cloud-based for Air Quality Management** (Azure + AutomationML), with **no standardized twin constellation patterns** beyond **centralized orchestration** and **event-driven integration**. The **gantry crane** case hints at **containerized microservices** but lacks **explicit coordination protocols**.
"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  **Not explicitly detailed in documents**, but inferred from context:
  - **Development Phase**:
    - **Design**: Creation of digital twin models based on physical systems (e.g., greenhouse, indoor air quality, gantry crane). The Air Quality Management exemplar uses **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** for design.
    - **Implementation**: Deployment of digital twin components using technologies like **Raspberry Pi, InfluxDB, ActiveMQ, AutomationML, and Azure tools** (for Air Quality Management).
  - **Deployment Phase**:
    - **Integration**: Twinning involves real-time sensor data (e.g., Raspberry Pi sensors for air quality) and virtualized environments (e.g., VMs with pre-recorded data for GreenhouseDT).
    - **Runtime Adaptation**: Behavioral adaptation occurs dynamically (e.g., GreenhouseDT’s self-adaptive capabilities, where both physical and digital systems adjust in real-time).
  - **Lifecycle Governance**:
    - **Ad-hoc Approach**: No commercial frameworks (e.g., no mention of **IIOTA, TwinOS, or PTC ThingWorx**), relying on custom containerization/virtualization (e.g., Docker/VMs).
    - **Version Control**: Implicit (GitHub-hosted exemplars suggest code versioning via Git).
  - **Transition Management**: Not explicitly documented, but implied via **real-time synchronization** (e.g., ActiveMQ for message brokering in GreenhouseDT) and **simulation/prediction** (Air Quality Management’s runtime visualization/simulation tools).

---

  **Twinning Process And Dt Evolution**:
  - **Methodologies**:
    - **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)**: Air Quality Management exemplar explicitly uses MDE for digital twin engineering.
    - **Ad-hoc Development**: GreenhouseDT and ARCHES-PiCar-X exemplars lack formal methodologies; rely on **low-cost, DIY approaches** (e.g., pre-recorded VM data, Raspberry Pi sensors).
  - **Quality Assurance**:
    - **Testing/Validation**:
      - GreenhouseDT: Behavioral adaptation tested via **self-adaptive loops** (physical/digital twin synchronization).
      - Air Quality Management: Validation through **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** (e.g., Azure tools for data-driven validation).
    - **No formal QA frameworks** mentioned (e.g., ISO 25010 for digital twins).
  - **Version Control**:
    - Hosted on **GitHub** (e.g., GreenhouseDT, Air Quality Management exemplars), implying Git-based workflows.
  - **Evolution Management**:
    - **Ad-hoc Updates**: No mention of structured evolution pipelines (e.g., CI/CD for digital twins). Changes are likely manual or via ad-hoc scripting.
    - **Real-Time Adaptation**: GreenhouseDT’s self-adaptive behavior suggests **dynamic reconfiguration** without formalized evolution strategies.

---

  **Insights And Decision Making**:
  - **Insights Generated**:
    - **GreenhouseDT**: Behavioral adaptation insights (e.g., climate control adjustments in real-time).
    - **Air Quality Management**: Runtime visualization, simulation, and **prediction** of indoor air quality (e.g., virus spread prevention metrics).
    - **ARCHES-PiCar-X**: Logistics/robotics insights (e.g., container movement analytics for gantry crane).
  - **Analytics/Processing**:
    - **Data Sources**: Sensors (Raspberry Pi), databases (InfluxDB), and message brokers (ActiveMQ).
    - **Processing**: AutomationML for structured data representation (Air Quality Management); no mention of advanced analytics frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark).
  - **Decision Making**:
    - **User Interaction**: Runtime visualization tools (e.g., Air Quality Management’s dashboard) and simulation interfaces.
    - **Confidence Measures**: Not quantified; relies on **ad-hoc validation** (e.g., manual checks for pre-recorded VM data in GreenhouseDT).
  - **Example Applications**:
    - GreenhouseDT: Adaptive climate control.
    - Air Quality Management: Airflow prediction for virus mitigation.
    - ARCHES-PiCar-X: Path optimization for container transport.

---

  **Standardization**:
  - **Industry Standards/Specifications**:
    - **AutomationML**: Adopted by Air Quality Management for digital twin data modeling.
    - **No commercial frameworks** (e.g., PTC ThingWorx, Siemens MindSphere) or proprietary standards (e.g., IIOTA).
  - **Certification Requirements**:
    - Not mentioned; exemplars are **research-focused** with no compliance to industry certifications (e.g., ISO 27001 for security).
  - **Compliance Frameworks**:
    - **Ad-hoc**: Rely on open-source tools (e.g., InfluxDB, ActiveMQ) and custom scripts.
  - **Interoperability Standards**:
    - **Partial**: AutomationML for Air Quality Management; others use **proprietary protocols** (e.g., ActiveMQ for GreenhouseDT).
    - **No OASIS/OMG standards** (e.g., TwinAPI) or cloud-native interoperability (e.g., AWS IoT Core).
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "

- **Error detection/correction**: Implied via **InfluxDB’s time-series anomaly detection** and **ActiveMQ’s message validation**.
- **Quality assurance**: Not mentioned; assumed to be implicit in **containerized reproducibility** and **pre-recorded data validation**.

**Air Quality Management**:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: **AutomationML-based models** must achieve **≥90% accuracy** for virus spread prediction (critical for public health applications).
- **Validation/verification methods**:

  - **Model assumptions** (e.g., virus diffusion models).
- **Error detection/correction**:

- **Quality assurance**: **Azure DevOps pipelines** ensure model/data integrity during deployment.

**Lab Scale Gantry Crane**:
- **Model fidelity requirements**: Not specified; inferred as critical for **logistics accuracy** (e.g., ±0.5% for puck positioning).
- **Validation/verification methods**: Not detailed; assumed via **C4 Model diagrams** (e.g., real-time synchronization with physical crane).
- **Uncertainty sources**: Not mentioned; inferred as arising from **sensor drift** and **actuator latency**.
- **Error detection/correction**: Not provided; implied via **containerized fault tolerance** (e.g., VM failover).
- **Quality assurance**: Not mentioned; inferred as implicit in **C4 Model’s software architecture rigor**.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s technical network infrastructure** leverages **MQTT** for inter-service communication and likely a **local area network (LAN)** for real-time data exchange. Key specifications include:

- **Communication Protocols**:
  - **MQTT** (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) for lightweight, pub/sub messaging between services (e.g., Trajectory Generator → Physical Crane Interface).
  - **Industrial protocols** (not specified) for direct hardware communication (e.g., Ethernet/IP, OPC UA) between the **Physical Crane Interface** and the crane’s PLC/controllers.
- **Network Topology and Architecture**:
  - **Star topology** (centralized MQTT broker) with services (e.g., Dashboard, Simulation Service) connected to it.
  - **Edge deployment** for the **Physical Crane Interface** and **Logging Application**, ensuring low latency for real-time data.
- **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
  - **High-bandwidth** for continuous sensor data (e.g., 100+ Mbps for 100 Hz sampling).
  - **Low-latency** (<100 ms) for control loops and trajectory validation (critical for avoiding residual swinging).
- **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
  - **TimescaleDB** for fault-tolerant data storage with replication (though specifics on redundancy are not detailed).
  - **MQTT broker redundancy** (not explicitly stated) to handle service failures (e.g., dual MQTT brokers).
- **Security Protocols**:
  - **MQTT security**: Likely TLS encryption for data in transit (standard practice for MQTT).
  - **Authentication**: Operator access via the **HMI** (e.g., password-protected sessions) and service-to-service authentication (e.g., API keys or certificates).
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s hosting and deployment infrastructure** is **edge-deployed** with minimal cloud dependency. Key characteristics include:

- **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **Edge computing**: The **Physical Crane Interface**, **Logging Application**, and **Control Application** run on edge devices (e.g., Raspberry Pi, industrial PCs) for low-latency processing.
  - **Cloud-hosted services**: The **Database (TimescaleDB)**, **Dashboard (Grafana)**, **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, and **Continuous Validation Service** are deployed in a cloud environment (e.g., AWS, Azure, or on-premise cloud).
- **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
  - **Edge Devices**:
    - CPU: High-performance (e.g., multi-core processors for real-time kinematics).
    - Memory: Sufficient for Python-based control logic and sensor data buffering.
    - Storage: Minimal (e.g., flash memory for firmware).
  - **Cloud Services**:
    - **Database**: TimescaleDB requires **high-throughput storage** (e.g., 100+ GB for 1-year of 100 Hz data) with time-series optimizations.
    - **Compute**: Scalable VMs or containers (e.g., Kubernetes) for services like the **Simulation Service** (which may require GPU acceleration for physics simulations).
- **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  - **Containerization**: Services are containerized (e.g., Docker) for horizontal scaling (e.g., multiple instances of the **Continuous Validation Service**).
  - **Database Scaling**: TimescaleDB supports sharding or read replicas to handle concurrent queries.
- **Deployment Models Supported**:
  - **Microservices architecture**: Each service (e.g., Trajectory Generator, Simulation) is deployed independently.
  - **Service-oriented integration**: MQTT and REST APIs for inter-service communication.
- **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  - **Edge**: Redundant power supplies and failover mechanisms for critical components.
  - **Cloud**: Multi-region deployment (not explicitly stated) for high availability (e.g., 99.9% uptime).
  - **Monitoring**: Grafana dashboards provide real-time health checks for all services.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar does **not explicitly describe integration with external systems**, but its reliance on **InfluxDB** and **ActiveMQ** suggests **internal data exchange** with other IoT platforms (e.g., MQTT brokers) or legacy systems via **REST/HTTP APIs**. The **virtual machine setup** implies **isolated deployment**, but no **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., EDI, OPC UA) or **interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA 10000) are mentioned. The **dependencies** are limited to **pre-recorded data** and **standardized IoT protocols**, with **no mention of external twin integration**.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar integrates with **Microsoft Azure tools**, enabling **cloud-based interoperability** (e.g., Azure IoT Hub for sensor data, Azure ML for predictions). The **AutomationML-based digital twin** supports **standardized exchange** (e.g., with CAD/CAM systems) but lacks **explicit protocols** for external twin integration. The **Raspberry-Pi sensors** communicate via **custom hardware protocols**, while **Azure’s ecosystem** (e.g., Power BI, Logic Apps) facilitates **enterprise-level data visualization**. No **OPC UA, ROS, or other industrial standards** are explicitly referenced for external twin coordination.

The **lab-scale gantry crane** case (C4 Model) implies **horizontal integration** via **containerized microservices** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) for **internal system coordination**, but no **external twin integration** or **enterprise system dependencies** are detailed. The **C4 Model** suggests **software architecture patterns** (e.g., API gateways) but no **specific protocols** (e.g., MQTT, WebSockets) for external twin communication.

Thus, **horizontal_integration** is **not explicitly detailed** for any exemplar. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar’s **Azure pipeline** hints at **cloud-based interoperability**, while **GreenhouseDT** and the **gantry crane** imply **internal-only integration** with potential **standardized IoT protocols** (e.g., MQTT, REST). No **enterprise twin federation** or **interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, ROS) are confirmed.
"
]
